heading: Emergency and transitional pet shelter and housing assistance grant program
label: 20127 Emergency and transitional pet shelter and housing assistance grant program

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary, acting in consultation with the Office of the Violence Against Women of the Department of Justice, the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development, and the Secretary of Health and Human Services, shall award grants under this section to eligible entities to carry out programs to provide the assistance described in paragraph (3) with respect to victims of domestic violence, dating violence, sexual assault, or stalking and the pets, service animals, emotional support animals, or horses of such victims.1So in original. Probably should be “Office on Violence Against Women”.1

children.heading: Memorandum of understanding
children.label: B Memorandum of understanding
children.text: The Secretary may enter into a memorandum of understanding with the head of another Department or agency, as appropriate, to carry out any of the authorities provided to the Secretary under this section.2See References in Text note below.2

children.label: i
children.text: a description of the activities for which a grant under this section is sought;

children.label: ii
children.text: such assurances as the Secretary determines to be necessary to ensure compliance by the entity with the requirements of this section; and

children.label: iii
children.text: a certification that the entity, before engaging with any individual domestic violence victim, will disclose to the victim any mandatory duty of the entity to report instances of abuse and neglect (including instances of abuse and neglect of pets, service animals, emotional support animals, or horses).

children.label: I
children.text: background checks of domestic violence victims; or

children.label: II
children.text: clinical evaluations to determine the eligibility of such a victim for support services;

children.label: ii
children.text: not include proposals that would require mandatory services for victims or that a victim obtain a protective order in order to receive proposed services; and

children.label: iii
children.text: reflect the eligible entity’s understanding of the dynamics of domestic violence, dating violence, sexual assault, or stalking.

children.label: i
children.text: domestic violence victims to participate in the criminal justice system in order to receive services; or

children.label: ii
children.text: eligible entities receiving a grant under this section to breach client confidentiality.

children.label: A
children.text: emergency and transitional shelter and housing assistance for domestic violence victims with pets, service animals, emotional support animals, or horses, including assistance with respect to any construction or operating expenses of newly developed or existing emergency and transitional pet, service animal, emotional support animal, or horse shelter and housing (regardless of whether such shelter and housing is co-located at a victim service provider or within the community);

children.label: B
children.text: short-term shelter and housing assistance for domestic violence victims with pets, service animals, emotional support animals, or horses, including assistance with respect to expenses incurred for the temporary shelter, housing, boarding, or fostering of the pets, service animals, emotional support animals, or horses of domestic violence victims and other expenses that are incidental to securing the safety of such a pet, service animal, emotional support animal, or horse during the sheltering, housing, or relocation of such victims;

children.label: I
children.text: safe housing with the victim’s pet, service animal, emotional support animal, or horse; or

children.label: II
children.text: safe accommodations for the victim’s pet, service animal, emotional support animal, or horse; or

children.label: ii
children.text: provide the victim with pet, service animal, emotional support animal, or horse related services, such as transportation, care services, and other assistance; or

children.label: i
children.text: the link between domestic violence, dating violence, sexual assault, or stalking and the abuse and neglect of pets, service animals, emotional support animals, and horses;

children.label: ii
children.text: the needs of domestic violence victims;

children.label: iii
children.text: best practices for providing support services to such victims;

children.label: iv
children.text: best practices for providing such victims with referrals to victims’ services; and

children.label: v
children.text: the importance of confidentiality.

children.label: A
children.text: to be bound by the nondisclosure of confidential information requirements of ; andsection 12291(b)(2) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: that the entity shall not condition the receipt of support, housing, or other benefits provided pursuant to this section on the participation of domestic violence victims in any or all of the support services offered to such victims through a program carried out by the entity using grant funds.

children.heading: Duration of assistance provided to victims
children.label: 5 Duration of assistance provided to victims

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), assistance provided with respect to a pet, service animal, emotional support animal, or horse of a domestic violence victim using grant funds awarded under this section shall be provided for a period of not more than 24 months.

children.label: i
children.text: has made a good faith effort to acquire permanent housing for the victim and the victim’s pet, service animal, emotional support animal, or horse during that 24-month period; and

children.label: ii
children.text: has been unable to acquire such permanent housing within that period.

children.label: A
children.text: the number of domestic violence victims with pets, service animals, emotional support animals, or horses provided such assistance; and

children.label: B
children.text: the purpose, amount, type of, and duration of such assistance.

children.heading: Reporting requirement
children.label: A Reporting requirement
children.text: Not later than November 1 of each even-numbered fiscal year, the Secretary shall submit to the Committee on Agriculture of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry of the Senate a report that contains a compilation of the information contained in the reports submitted under paragraph (6).

children.label: i
children.text: the Office on Violence Against Women of the Department of Justice;

children.label: ii
children.text: the Office of Community Planning and Development of the Department of Housing and Urban Development; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the Administration for Children and Families of the Department of Health and Human Services.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section $3,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2019 through 2023.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: B Limitation
children.text: Of the amount made available under subparagraph (A) in any fiscal year, not more than 5 percent may be used for evaluation, monitoring, salaries, and administrative expenses.

children.heading: Domestic violence victim defined
children.label: A Domestic violence victim defined
children.text: The term “domestic violence victim” means a victim of domestic violence, dating violence, sexual assault, or stalking.

children.label: i
children.text: a State;

children.label: ii
children.text: a unit of local government;

children.label: iii
children.text: an Indian tribe; or

children.label: I
children.text: a domestic violence and sexual assault victim service provider;

children.label: II
children.text: a domestic violence and sexual assault coalition;

children.label: III
children.text: a community-based and culturally specific organization;

children.label: IV
children.text: any other nonprofit, nongovernmental organization; and

children.label: aa
children.text: an animal shelter; and

children.label: bb
children.text: an animal welfare organization.

children.heading: Emotional support animal
children.label: C Emotional support animal
children.text: The term “emotional support animal” means an animal that is covered by the exclusion specified in section 5.303 of title 24, Code of Federal Regulations (or a successor regulation), and that is not a service animal.

children.heading: Pet
children.label: D Pet
children.text: The term “pet” means a domesticated animal, such as a dog, cat, bird, rodent, fish, turtle, or other animal that is kept for pleasure rather than for commercial purposes.

children.heading: Service animal
children.label: E Service animal
children.text: The term “service animal” has the meaning given the term in section 36.104 of title 28, Code of Federal Regulations (or a successor regulation).

children.heading: Other terms
children.label: F Other terms
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in this section, terms used in this section shall have the meaning given such terms in .2section 12291(a) of this title
